title: malus microcarpa sempervirens
intro: Malus microcarpa sempervirens, also known as the Chinese crabapple, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family Rosaceae. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China, valued for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The plant is used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, headache, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of Malus microcarpa sempervirens include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to soothe coughs, reduce swelling, and promote overall well-being.
